items.headline: City increases budget for South Bay water recycling pump station
items.summary: The City Council approved a construction contingency increase for the South Bay Water Recycling Pump Station 5 Bypass Project on April 14. This water infrastructure upgrade supports regional water recycling and supply resilience.

items.headline: City Council reviews 2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: The council held a study session on the Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines major infrastructure and facility projects the city plans to fund. This is where residents can learn what physical improvements or construction projects Mountain View is prioritizing for the coming year.

items.headline: Sunnyvale proposes changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance amending the Residential Tenant Protections Program, which could affect how landlords and renters are regulated in Sunnyvale. Details on what protections are being added or modified would impact current and future renters in the city.

items.headline: Downtown building gets historic designation upgrade and bonus
items.summary: The City Council is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street from Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource status, which would grant an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area for rehabilitation work. This incentivizes preservation of older downtown buildings.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed for San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone and redevelop property at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road into an eight-story building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This is a significant project that would change the character of that commercial corridor.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: An administrative zoning hearing scheduled for Wednesday, April 8 was cancelled and rescheduled to April 22. A project at 333 Franklin Street will not be heard on the original date and will be re-noticed once a new hearing date is set.

items.headline: City awards contract for 2026 street pavement repairs
items.summary: San José awarded a construction contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project on April 7, addressing road maintenance across the city. This project directly affects neighborhood street conditions and transportation infrastructure.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue highway-rail traffic signal project advances
items.summary: The city extended its consultant agreement with DKS Associates to continue work on the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7. This project manages safety where cars and trains intersect, affecting commute patterns in that corridor.
